_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: File I/O
summary: Read and write files through streams.
refresher: C++ uses file streams for file work. `std::ifstream` reads from a file. `std::ofstream` writes to a file. Always check that the stream opened before reading or writing.

blocks.body: A file stream is a path between your program and a file. Use an input file stream to read. Use an output file stream to write.
Opening a file can fail. The file may be in another folder, the name may be misspelled, or the program may not have permission. Check the stream before you trust it.

blocks.title: Compare this with Python's open
blocks.body: Python often uses `with open(...) as file`. C++ creates an `ifstream` or `ofstream` object. Both approaches close the file automatically when the surrounding block finishes.

blocks.caption: The program checks that the file opened, then prints each line only after getline succeeds.
blocks.output: One output line for each line in courses.txt.

blocks.caption: EOF becomes known only after a read fails. Testing getline directly prevents one extra pass with old or empty data.

blocks.body: The final real line is processed normally. The next call to `getline` fails because there is nothing left to read, so the condition becomes false and the loop ends before stale data can be used.

blocks.title: Relative paths start at the working directory
blocks.body: A path such as `courses.txt` starts from the program's current working directory, which may not be the folder containing the source file. Check the run configuration or use a known path when a file exists but cannot be found.

blocks.prompt: Which stream type reads from a file?
blocks.why: An ifstream is an input file stream, while an ofstream sends output into a file.

choices: The body runs only after a successful read
It reads the entire file at once
It keeps the stream open forever
blocks.why: The returned stream state becomes false when reading fails, preventing the loop from processing invalid data.

blocks.title: File I/O checklist
items: Read line by line when the file is text.
Check the working folder when the file is missing.
